question: Will the price of Ethereum be between $3,100 and $3,200 on January 31?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

description:

This market will resolve to "Sentinels" if Sentinels secures 10 or more kills than Disguised in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Disguised".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Aarhus GF vs. Odense BK: O/U 1.5
description:
In the upcoming Denmark Superliga game between Aarhus GF and Odense BK, scheduled for February 6 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Aarhus GF and Odense BK combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on superliga.dk. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Solana Up or Down - January 26, 1PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: XRP Up or Down - January 26, 1PM ET
description:

question: International T20 Series: India vs New Zealand (Game 5)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between India and New Zealand scheduled for January 31 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: India vs New Zealand (Game 5) - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between India and New Zealand scheduled for 2026-01-31 in India T20 Series.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: International T20 Series: India vs New Zealand (Game 5) - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between India and New Zealand scheduled for 2026-01-31 in India T20 Series is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

question: International T20 Series: India vs New Zealand (Game 5) - Toss Match Double India Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between India and New Zealand scheduled for 2026-01-31 in India T20 Series.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to India will be considered correct if  India is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: U19 World Cup: Australia Under-19s vs South Africa Under-19s
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Australia Under-19s and South Africa Under-19s scheduled for January 25 2026 in U19 World Cup.
This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  
DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.
If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

question: WPL: Gujarat Giants Women vs Delhi Capitals Women
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Gujarat Giants Women and Delhi Capitals Women scheduled for January 27 2026 in WPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: WPL: Gujarat Giants Women vs Delhi Capitals Women - Toss Match Double Gujarat Giants Women Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Gujarat Giants Women and Delhi Capitals Women scheduled for 2026-01-27.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Gujarat Giants Women will be considered correct if  Gujarat Giants Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Delhi Capitals Women will be considered correct if Delhi Capitals Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: WPL: Gujarat Giants Women vs Delhi Capitals Women - Most Sixes Gujarat Giants Women Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Gujarat Giants Women and Delhi Capitals Women scheduled for 2026-01-27.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Gujarat Giants Women will be considered correct if Gujarat Giants Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Delhi Capitals Women.The outcome corresponding to Delhi Capitals Women will be considered correct if Delhi Capitals Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Gujarat Giants Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: WPL: UP Warriorz Women vs Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between UP Warriorz Women and Royal Challengers Bengaluru Women scheduled for January 29 2026 in WPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Gujarat Giants Women and Mumbai Indians Women scheduled for January 30 2026 in WPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.

description:

question: Women's T20 World Cup Qualifier: USA Women vs Papua New Guinea Women - Toss Match Double USA Women Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between USA Women and Papua New Guinea Women scheduled for 2026-01-25.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to USA Women will be considered correct if  USA Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Papua New Guinea Women will be considered correct if Papua New Guinea Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between USA Women and Papua New Guinea Women scheduled for 2026-01-25.

The outcome corresponding to USA Women will be considered correct if USA Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Papua New Guinea Women.The outcome corresponding to Papua New Guinea Women will be considered correct if Papua New Guinea Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than USA Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Netherlands Women and Thailand Women scheduled for 2026-01-26.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Netherlands Women will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Netherlands Women.
The outcome corresponding to Thailand Women will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Thailand Women.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Women's T20 World Cup Qualifier: Nepal Women vs Scotland Women
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Nepal Women and Scotland Women scheduled for January 25 2026 in Women's T20 World Cup Qualifier.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

description:

question: Women's T20 World Cup Qualifier: Nepal Women vs Scotland Women - Toss Match Double Nepal Women Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Nepal Women and Scotland Women scheduled for 2026-01-25.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Nepal Women will be considered correct if  Nepal Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Scotland Women will be considered correct if Scotland Women is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: Women's T20 World Cup Qualifier: Nepal Women vs Scotland Women - Most Sixes Nepal Women Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Nepal Women and Scotland Women scheduled for 2026-01-25.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Nepal Women will be considered correct if Nepal Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Scotland Women.The outcome corresponding to Scotland Women will be considered correct if Scotland Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Nepal Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Bhutan Tri-Series: Bhutan Women vs Hong Kong Women - Most Sixes Bhutan Women Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Bhutan Women and Hong Kong Women scheduled for 2026-01-24.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Bhutan Women will be considered correct if Bhutan Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hong Kong Women.The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong Women will be considered correct if Hong Kong Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Bhutan Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Bhutan Tri-Series: Hong Kong Women vs Malaysia Women - Most Sixes Hong Kong Women Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Hong Kong Women and Malaysia Women scheduled for 2026-01-25.

The outcome corresponding to Hong Kong Women will be considered correct if Hong Kong Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Malaysia Women.The outcome corresponding to Malaysia Women will be considered correct if Malaysia Women is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Hong Kong Women.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 26, 2PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Counter-Strike: SemperFi Esports vs HAVU (BO3) - United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between SemperFi Esports and HAVU in the United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs, scheduled for January 24 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "SemperFi Esports" if SemperFi Esports win the match against HAVU.

This market will resolve to "HAVU" if HAVU win the match against SemperFi Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

This market refers to the Counter-Strike match between SemperFi Esports and HAVU in the United21 x Esplay Challenge Playoffs, scheduled for January 24 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if SemperFi Esports and HAVU play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.sofascore.com/esports.

description:
